diet: Unlimited grass hay (Timothy/Orchard) + leafy greens daily + limited pellets (≈1 tbsp/kg/day; max ⅛ cup per 2 kg adult; reduce in obese). NO fruit treats > 1 tbsp/day. NO seeds, nuts, grains.
housing: Min total enclosure 3 m × 2 m × 1 m (~60 sqft) including permanent run (RWAF 2023). Hutch ≥1.8 m long. Solid floor (no wire). Free-roam preferred. Paper-based litter.
common: ⚠ RHDV2 (vaccine-preventable, ≥70% mortality), GI stasis, dental disease (cheek teeth elongation), urinary sludge, sore hocks, head tilt (E. cuniculi / otitis media), uterine adenocarcinoma in intact does
mistakes: Not vaccinating against RHDV2 + myxomatosis, wire bottom cage, pellet-heavy diet, no hay, no spay → uterine adenocarcinoma ~50–80 % cumulative by age 5–6, bathing (rabbits don't need baths)
redflags: ⚠ ANOREXIA > 12h = EMERGENCY (GI stasis). ⚠ Reduced fecal output = emergency. ⚠ Head tilt + nystagmus = E. cuniculi, otitis media (incl. Pasteurella multocida).

diet: Unlimited hay + leafy greens (vit-C rich: bell pepper, parsley) + vit-C-fortified pellets. ⚠ DAILY VITAMIN C 10–30 mg/kg mandatory (cannot synthesize).
housing: Min 7.5 sqft for 1, ≥10.5 (ideally ≥13) for 2, ≥30 sqft for 4+. Solid floor. SOCIAL species — pairs minimum (RSPCA / Cavy Care 2023).
common: Scurvy (vit C deficiency), pododermatitis, ovarian cysts (entire females), URI, dental malocclusion
mistakes: Single housing (social isolation), no daily vit C, pellet without vit C, wire bottom cage causing bumblefoot
redflags: ⚠ Anorexia = emergency. ⚠ Hair loss + dyspnea in older female = ovarian cyst. ⚠ Pregnancy in females > 8 months never bred = dystocia risk (pelvic symphysis fuses).

diet: Obligate carnivore. High-quality ferret kibble OR raw/whole prey diet. ⚠ NO fruit, vegetables, grains, dairy.
housing: Multi-level cage with hammocks. Ferret-proof room for daily 4h+ play. Smell — clean litter daily.
common: Adrenal disease, insulinoma, lymphoma, dental disease, GI foreign body, canine distemper virus (CDV — vaccine-preventable, 100% fatal), influenza (catches from humans)
mistakes: Sugar treats (cause insulinoma), no vaccine, exposing to puppies (CDV), exposing to humans with flu, allowing chew of rubber/foam
redflags: ⚠ Hypoglycemia (drooling, ataxia, seizure) = insulinoma crisis — Karo syrup PO + emergency. ⚠ Hair loss / vulvar swelling in spayed female = adrenal disease. ⚠ Vomiting + decreased appetite = often GI FB (rubber, hair tie) → surgery.

temp: 15–21 °C ideal; heat STRESS > 25 °C, high heat-stroke risk > 27 °C (no sweat glands; humid heat much worse — aim heat index < 30)
diet: Unlimited grass hay + chinchilla pellets (NOT rabbit pellets — too high protein/fat). Treats: 1 raisin/wk max. NO nuts, seeds.
housing: Tall multi-level wire cage with solid wood shelves (chew, ledge). DUST BATH 2–3×/week (chinchilla dust, not sand). Quiet area — nocturnal.
common: Heat stroke, dental disease (very common — slobbers = malocclusion), fur slip (handling stress), bumblefoot, GI stasis
mistakes: Heat exposure (room temp > 27 °C dangerous, humid heat worse), water bath (causes fungal — only dust), single housing (social), wire bottom shelves
redflags: ⚠ Hyperthermia > 38.5 °C = emergency cool down. ⚠ Drooling (slobbers) = dental disease. ⚠ Anorexia = emergency (similar to rabbit GI stasis).

temp: Aim 24–27 °C (PETR 24–29 °C per Carpenter 6e p.522 Table 8-11); heat stress > 28 °C; torpor risk < 18 °C = EMERGENCY
uvb: Low UVB may be beneficial (limited evidence — crepuscular not strictly nocturnal)
diet: Quality cat kibble + insects (mealworms, crickets) + small bits of cooked egg / meat. Limit fruit. Hedgehog kibbles often inadequate.
housing: Min 4 sqft solid-floor enclosure. Wheel (solid surface, ≥ 28 cm) mandatory — nocturnal runners. Hide. Heating pad / lamp to maintain 24+°C.
common: Wobbly Hedgehog Syndrome (WHS — degenerative neurological, fatal), obesity, dental disease, mites (Caparinia), squamous cell carcinoma (oral)
mistakes: Cold environment (induces hibernation attempt = fatal), wire wheel (foot trauma), no wheel at all (obesity), single feeding insects (calcium imbalance)
redflags: ⚠ Hibernation attempt (cold, lethargic, curled tight) = warm gradually + emergency exam. ⚠ Wobbly gait progressing = WHS, no treatment. ⚠ Bloody stool / urine = often urolithiasis.

uvb: Low UVB may be beneficial (limited evidence)
diet: HPW (Wombaroo high-protein) or BML-style diet + gut-loaded insects + small fruit/veg + Ca/D3 supplement. ⚠ Leadbeater's mix is OUTDATED (high vit A, imbalanced Ca:P). Calcium-deficient diet → HLP (hind limb paralysis).
housing: TALL flight cage min 90 cm × 60 cm × 90 cm. Vertical climbing space. Nest box high in cage. Must be kept in PAIRS or colonies — social species.
common: Hind limb paralysis (calcium deficiency), self-mutilation (single housing stress), penile prolapse (intact males), obesity, dental disease
mistakes: Single housing → severe self-mutilation, low-calcium diet (apple/banana-only), no UVB or D3, daytime disturbance (nocturnal)
redflags: ⚠ Hind limb weakness = MBD/hypocalcemia — calcium gluconate emergency. ⚠ Self-mutilation = stress/isolation — pair up. ⚠ Bonded glider rejected → may mutilate self.

temp: 21–35 °C ambient gradient (Carpenter 6e Table 4-19); basking 38–43 °C; night dip to 21–24 °C OK
uvb: 10.0 UVB tube full length — replace q6m
diet: Juveniles ~70% insect / 30% greens BY VOLUME, fed 2–3×/day; Adults ~20% insect / 80% greens, fed 1×/day. Insects: crickets, dubia, BSF larvae. Dust with calcium daily + multivit 2x/wk. NO fireflies, lightning bugs (toxic).
housing: Min 120 × 60 × 60 cm for adult. Strict basking spot 38–43 °C. Loose substrate (sand) controversial — newspaper/tile safer (impaction risk).
common: Metabolic bone disease (MBD), atadenovirus, yellow fungus disease (Nannizziopsis guarroi; formerly CANV), impaction, parasites (coccidia, pinworm), prolapse
mistakes: No UVB / expired UVB bulb, no calcium supplement, loose sand substrate (impaction), single insect diet, no basking spot
redflags: ⚠ Soft jaw / fractured limb spontaneously = MBD — calcium IM emergency. ⚠ Yellow scales = yellow fungus (poor prognosis). ⚠ Lethargy + brumation in winter normal but verify weight stable.

temp: Cool end 24–27 °C, warm end 30–32 °C, basking ~32 °C (Carpenter 6e Table 4-19); night drop to 22–24 °C OK
humidity: 70–80 % ambient with humidity box; ≥80 % during shed (Carpenter 6e Table 4-19)
diet: Frozen-thawed mouse / rat 1 prey item q1–2 wks (juvenile) or q2–4 wks (adult). NEVER live prey (injury risk to snake).
housing: Tub or tank min 120 cm length. 2 hides (warm + cool), water bowl big enough to soak. Substrate: aspen, coconut fiber, paper.
common: Respiratory infection (cold + damp), mites (Ophionyssus natricis), inclusion body disease (boid), anorexia (often husbandry-related), dysecdysis
mistakes: Live prey feeding, wrong temp gradient, no humidity during shed (incomplete sheds), excessive handling, glass tank without secure cover
redflags: ⚠ Open-mouth breathing + mucus = RI emergency. ⚠ Star-gazing / corkscrewing = neurological — IBD, vitamin def. ⚠ Anorexia in winter often normal brumation; verify weight loss < 10%.

diet: STRICT herbivore. Grasses, hay, leafy greens (dandelion, mulberry, plantain). Limit fruit < 5% diet. Calcium block + cuttlebone.
housing: Outdoor pen ideal in warm months. Indoor: HUGE tortoise table or room — NOT a glass tank (cannot see boundary, paces). Hide, water dish to soak.
common: Metabolic bone disease, shell pyramiding (low humidity + poor diet), hypervitaminosis A (over-supplementation), bladder stones, respiratory infection
mistakes: Dog/cat food protein (causes pyramiding + kidney damage), iceberg lettuce (low nutrition), glass tank (stress + reflection), no UVB / expired bulb
redflags: ⚠ Soft shell / spontaneous fracture = MBD. ⚠ Anorexia + nasal discharge + URI = often respiratory infection requiring abx. ⚠ Pyramided shell already permanent — focus on preventing further.

diet: Pellet diet 70% + seeds 15% + fresh fruit/veg 15%. ⚠ Seed-only diet = malnutrition, fatty liver, atherosclerosis. NO avocado, chocolate, caffeine, alcohol.
housing: Min 60 × 40 × 40 cm budgie, 75 × 60 × 75 cockatiel. Out-of-cage time daily. NO TEFLON cookware in home — fumes lethal.
common: Egg-binding (egg-laying females), goiter (iodine deficiency in budgies), psittacosis (Chlamydia), polyomavirus / PBFD (esp. budgies), PDD-bornavirus (cockatiels), feather destructive behavior, fatty liver disease, gout
mistakes: Seed-only diet, Teflon pan in kitchen, scented candles / air fresheners, no out-of-cage time, no UV light, mirror toys (encourage hormonal behavior)
redflags: ⚠ Fluffed + tail bobbing + sleepy = SICK BIRD = emergency. ⚠ Open-beak breathing = severe respiratory distress. ⚠ Sudden death after using new pan = Teflon fume toxicity.

uvb: Bird-safe full-spectrum UVB 4–8 h/day (not 12 h continuous — risks ocular damage)
diet: Pellet base + extensive fresh vegetables + small fruit. Nuts in shell (almond/walnut) only as occasional foraging enrichment (high fat → contributes to atherosclerosis if free-fed). CALCIUM critical — Greys prone to hypocalcemia.
housing: Min 90 × 75 × 90 cm. Large play stand outside cage. Mental enrichment essential — forage toys, training time daily.
common: Hypocalcemia / seizures, feather plucking (stress / boredom), atherosclerosis, aspergillosis, PBFD, heavy metal toxicity
mistakes: Seed-only diet (hypocalcemia), insufficient mental enrichment (plucking), Teflon fumes, allowing chew of zinc-galvanized cage parts (heavy metal)
redflags: ⚠ Sudden seizure in adult Grey = hypocalcemia until proven otherwise — calcium gluconate IM. ⚠ Plucking = behavioral + medical workup. ⚠ Vomiting + crop stasis = often Macrorhabdus or candida.

temp: Cold-acclimated breeds tolerate −5 to 35 °C with shelter; tropical layer breeds intolerant of cold. Heat stress > 32 °C
diet: Layer pellets (16% protein) + scratch grains + free-range greens / insects. Calcium oyster shell free-choice for laying hens.
housing: Coop min 4 sqft/bird + 10 sqft/bird run. Roost bars, nesting boxes (1 per 4 hens), predator-proof.
common: Marek's disease (prevented ONLY by day-old hatchery vaccination — order vaccinated chicks), respiratory disease (CRD/Mycoplasma), egg-binding, vent prolapse, internal lay, lice/mites, coccidiosis (chicks)
mistakes: Buying unvaccinated chicks (Marek's vaccine must be given at hatchery — owners cannot effectively vaccinate adults), overcrowding, no calcium → soft shell eggs, mixing with wild birds, predator-vulnerable coop
redflags: ⚠ Egg-bound hen straining > 12h = emergency (calcium gluconate + warm bath + manual reduction). ⚠ Sudden respiratory distress in flock = isolate + culture. ⚠ Withdrawal times mandatory on all medications for laying hens — record carefully.
